name: Anubis Jackals
description: Anubis Jackals are creatures deeply intertwined with the jackal-headed god Anubis, guardian of the dead and protector of the afterlife. They are often depicted as either literal jackals with heightened intelligence and supernatural abilities, or as anthropomorphic jackal-headed beings serving Anubis. Their primary role is to assist Anubis in his duties, guarding tombs, weighing the hearts of the deceased against the feather of Ma'at, and guiding souls through the perilous journey to the underworld. Some accounts portray them as fearsome protectors, fiercely loyal to Anubis and merciless towards those who would desecrate sacred burial grounds or attempt to cheat the judgment of the dead. Others describe them as compassionate guides, comforting lost souls and ensuring their safe passage. Their appearance and abilities can vary depending on the specific myth, but their connection to Anubis and their association with death and the afterlife remain constant. Anubis Jackals are more than just animals; they are extensions of Anubis' will, embodying his wisdom, justice, and unwavering commitment to maintaining the balance between life and death. Their presence serves as a constant reminder of the mortality of humankind and the importance of living a virtuous life in preparation for the final judgment.
subtitle: Guardians of the Dead's Domain
appearance: Anubis Jackals typically resemble jackals, but often larger and more imposing, with glowing eyes and an aura of otherworldly power. Some are depicted with human-like bodies and jackal heads, clad in traditional Egyptian attire or armor. Their fur ranges from black to golden, symbolizing the darkness of the underworld and the preciousness of the afterlife. They can also manifest as shadows or spectral forms, able to phase through walls and sense the presence of the dead.
habitat: Anubis Jackals primarily dwell in and around tombs, burial grounds, and the Duat (the Egyptian underworld). They are also found in desolate desert landscapes, where they act as guardians against grave robbers and those who would disturb the peace of the dead. Their presence is most strongly felt in places where the veil between the mortal world and the afterlife is thin.
behavior: Anubis Jackals are fiercely loyal to Anubis and dedicated to their duties as guardians and guides. They are intelligent and cunning, able to anticipate threats and adapt to changing circumstances. While they can be intimidating and even fearsome to those who approach with ill intentions, they are also capable of compassion and empathy, especially towards lost or suffering souls. They operate with a strong sense of justice and uphold the laws of Ma'at, ensuring that the balance between good and evil is maintained.

keyMyths.title: Guiding Souls to the Afterlife
keyMyths.description: Anubis Jackals guide the souls of the deceased through the treacherous landscape of the Duat, protecting them from demons and ensuring they reach the Hall of Judgement. They serve as intermediaries between the mortal world and the afterlife.

keyMyths.title: Guardians of the Tombs
keyMyths.description: Anubis Jackals are sworn protectors of tombs and burial grounds, fiercely defending them against grave robbers and desecrators. They use their supernatural abilities to deter intruders and punish those who violate the sacred space.
keyMyths.source: Various tomb inscriptions and funerary texts

sources.author: E.A. Wallis Budge
sources.description: A comprehensive translation and analysis of the ancient Egyptian funerary text.
